summary: Obfuscated import-time dropper in scoped typosquat of treeify
details: The package republishes the legitimate `treeify` source (lines 1-113 of treeify.js, retaining original author metadata `Luke Plaster <notatestuser@gmail.com>` and repo `notatestuser/treeify.git`) under the unrelated `@osmura` scope, then appends ~93KB of obfuscator.io-style code (string-array decoder `a4()`, RC4 routine `a5()`, two IIFEs) at line 115+. On `require('@osmura/treeify')` the appended code runs at import time: it sets a re-entry environment variable, re-spawns the current process detached, derives a download URL by HMAC/AES-decrypting hardcoded base64 constants (so no destination appears as a plain string), performs an HTTP/HTTPS GET on port 443/0x1bb following 301/302/303/307/308 redirects, verifies a SHA-256, writes the response into `os.tmpdir()`, chmods it 0o755, and executes it via `child_process` with `detached: true`. The legitimate API surface (asLines/asTree/growBranch) is preserved so consumers observe expected behavior while attacker-controlled bytes execute as the current user. The combination of author-identity impersonation, scoped name confusion against the popular `treeify` package, dynamically-derived (non-static) C2 destination, and import-time fetch-decrypt-write-execute chain is an active supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
